items.title: Asian Oil Buyers in Talks With Saudis to Reroute Red Sea Flows
items.description: At least two Asian crude oil buyers are in talks with Saudi Aramco to potentially reroute flows around Africa after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea, according to traders familiar with the mat

items.title: Victoria Plc Wins Support for Debt Deal After HSBC U-Turn
items.description: Troubled UK carpetmaker Victoria Plc secured enough bondholder support for a consensual debt restructuring after HSBC Holdings Plc backed the plan at the last minute, averting a costlier, more time-co

items.title: BlackRock Says Elevated Yields Offer Bond Investors a Cushion
items.description: US Treasuries are offering investors solid protection against losses as yields remain elevated, according to BlackRock Inc.

items.title: PHP/USD: Marcos Confident Central Bank to Act on Weak Peso, Official Says
items.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. is confident that the central bank will act decisively on the peso, which weakened to the record low, according to the president’s press officer.

items.title: German Bond Yields Hit 15-Year High as Oil Spikes Before ECB
items.description: German bond yields rose to the highest level since 2011 as surging energy prices and mounting inflation expectations fueled bets on interest-rate hikes ahead of the European Central Bank’s decision la

items.title: Mark Gurman on Apple's (AAPL) Upcoming Product Roadmap
items.description: Mark Gurman on the state of play.

items.title: Odd Lots: Why Apple Is Heading Into a Pivotal Moment (Podcast)
items.description: There used to be a time when the latest product release from Apple was a huge deal. But lately, it’s been kind of ‘meh,’ and a lot of the hype in the tech world has been directed at companies doing th

items.title: From Defense to Housing, Luxembourg Steps Up Retail Bonds Push
items.description: Luxembourg’s successful sale of defense bonds has encouraged it to again turn to retail investors, this time with securities that will help fund affordable housing.

items.title: China Tankers Reach Red Sea Chokepoint Despite Houthi Threat
items.description: Two China-owned oil tankers loaded with Saudi Arabian crude remained on course to exit the Red Sea via the Bab el-Mandeb chokepoint, despite overnight Houthi attacks on vessels in the region.

items.title: EasyJet Says Customers are Resuming More Normal Booking Patterns
items.description: EasyJet Plc said customers are becoming more confident to book longer in advance again after resorting to last-minute purchases out of concern of cancelations, helping the low-cost carrier improve pre

items.title: TotalEnergies Profit Jumps 68% as Wars Drive Refining Boom
items.description: TotalEnergies SE said second-quarter earnings surged as wars in Iran and Ukraine boosted prices for crude and refined products, offsetting a drop in profit from its gas business.

items.title: Airtel Mobile-Money Unit Picks London for Listing Location
items.description: Airtel Africa Plc picked London as the primary listing location for its mobile-money business and expects to start trading there this year.

items.title: Roche Quarterly Sales Held Back by Blockbuster Eye Drug
items.description: Roche Holding’s results showed a slower-than-expected recovery for eye blockbuster Vabysmo as investors look to a key new drug to power growth ahead.

items.title: BNP’s Stock Traders Ride Wall Street Rally With 43% Gain
items.description: BNP Paribas SA reported better-than-expected results for the second quarter as its stock traders blew past analysts’ estimates, in a sign that the rally that fueled record results on Wall Street spill

items.title: Zimbabwe’s State Firm Secures $300 Million to Develop Lithium Mine
items.description: Mutapa Energy Resources, Zimbabwe’s state-owned lithium miner, has secured $300 million from a group of companies, which includes Chinese entities, to develop its assets in the southern African nation

items.title: Indonesian Stocks Briefly Near Bull Market as Sentiment Steadies
items.description: A brief rally in Indonesian stocks pushed the benchmark index to the cusp of a bull market, as investors rotated into laggards and concerns over the country’s fiscal health eased.

items.title: Stocks Fall as AI Spending Worries Grow, Oil Rises: Markets Wrap
items.description: Stocks fell as unease over ever-higher artificial intelligence spending outweighed an otherwise strong earnings report from Alphabet Inc., while crude rose for a fifth straight day and pushed bond yie

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 23
items.description: Oil closed in on $100 a barrel in London after Iran-backed Houthi militants said they attacked two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea, escalating the Middle East conflict and threatening deeper supp

items.title: Aston Martin Raises £550 Million Debt From HPS to Boost Cash
items.description: Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc has struck a deal for £550 million ($736 million) of fresh funds with BlackRock Inc.-owned HPS Investment Partners to bolster its liquidity.

items.title: Heat and War Deal Europe’s Grains Biggest Blow in Decades
items.description: Europe is set to suffer one of its sharpest ever declines in grain production as extreme weather and geopolitical tensions converge to pile pressure on farmers.
